verification_notes: 18 holes, par 72, 6,380 yards confirmed. Year 1986 confirmed. Located Lak Hok, Pathum Thani — original and oldest of the three Muang Ake Group courses. Green fees 610–1,030 THB range (1golf.eu) — all-inclusive package; using 610 THB weekday, 1,030 THB weekend midrange. Official website muangakegolf.co.th exists. Marked provisional as exact weekday/weekend split from official source not confirmed.
disputes: Green fee range of 610–1,030 THB (18 holes) from 1golf.eu may reflect time-of-day or season variation rather than a clean weekday/weekend split. Figures used are the reported low/high endpoints.

prose.overview: Muang Ake Golf Course is the original and oldest of the three golf courses developed by the Muang Ake Group in Pathum Thani province, north of Bangkok. Opened in 1986, it occupies a prime position just off the Phahonyothin Highway in Lak Hok — one of the most accessible locations of any 18-hole course in the greater Bangkok area, roughly 30 kilometres from the city centre. The course is a parkland layout with a notably shorter yardage (6,380 yards) compared to many Bangkok-area courses, making it genuinely welcoming to higher handicappers, beginners, and golfers looking for an enjoyable round without the pressure of a demanding championship layout. Its very affordable green fees make it popular among local Thai golfers and expats seeking regular weekday play, and the proximity to Don Mueang Airport (approximately 10 minutes) makes it a convenient first- or last-day option for travellers transiting through Thailand.
prose.layout_and_experience: The 18-hole par-72 layout stretches 6,380 yards from the back tees, playing shorter from the middle markers — a flat, tree-lined parkland course in the classic Thai tradition. Fairways are generous and the rough is manageable, placing the primary emphasis on approach accuracy and putting rather than driving power. Water features on several holes add interest without creating unfair penalisation. The greens, while not at championship speed, putt consistently and reward well-struck approaches. Because the course is popular with regular local players, caddies know every green slope intimately and their advice on club selection and line is usually reliable. The calm, unhurried atmosphere — a deliberate product of sensible tee-time spacing — makes for an enjoyable round at a pace that Bangkok's busier courses rarely achieve.
prose.tips: At this price point, Muang Ake Golf Course is exceptional value and worth booking even on a casual schedule. Arrive early on weekday mornings for the freshest greens and quietest experience. The course is directly off Phahonyothin Highway, making it easy to reach from central Bangkok or Don Mueang Airport without a GPS. Caddie fees are included in the green fee package; a tip of 200–300 THB is customary. Clubs for hire are available on-site at modest cost. The course is also a convenient warm-up round before attempting one of the more demanding courses in the Muang Ake complex.
prose.location_and_access: Muang Ake Golf Course is located at 52 Soi Phahonyothin, Lak Hok, Mueang Pathum Thani, Pathum Thani, approximately 30 km north of central Bangkok. Travel time is around 35 minutes via the Phahonyothin Highway (Highway 1). Don Mueang Airport is approximately 10 minutes south. Public transport is limited; minibus services from Mo Chit station run along Phahonyothin but the club requires a short taxi or Grab from the main road.
prose.rental_cta_context: Muang Ake is one of Bangkok's most accessible courses — and with LENGOLF club rentals delivered to your hotel, you can be on the first tee in under 40 minutes from the city without dragging your bag through Bangkok traffic.
